Description: [Omitted by the Bangladesh Laws (Revision and Declaration) Act, 1973 (Act No. VIII of 1973), section 3 and 2nd Schedule.]
Period of limitation: 1. [Omitted by the Bangladesh Laws (Revision and Declaration) Act, 1973 (Act No. VIII of 1973), section 3 and 2nd Schedule.]
Time from which period begins to run: 1. [Omitted by the Bangladesh Laws (Revision and Declaration) Act, 1973 (Act No. VIII of 1973), section 3 and 2nd Schedule.]

Description: For compensation for doing or for omitting to do an act alleged to be in pursuance of any enactment in force for the time being in Bangladesh.
Time from which period begins to run: When the act or omission takes place.

Description: Under the Specific Relief Act, 1877, section 9, to recover possession of immoveable property.
Time from which period begins to run: When the dispossession occurs.

Description: [Omitted by the by the Repealing and Amending Act, 1937 (Act No. XX of 1937), section 3 and Second Schedule.]
Period of limitation: 4. [Omitted by the by the Repealing and Amending Act, 1937 (Act No. XX of 1937), section 3 and Second Schedule.]
Time from which period begins to run: 4. [Omitted by the by the Repealing and Amending Act, 1937 (Act No. XX of 1937), section 3 and Second Schedule.]

Description: Under the summary procedure referred to in section 128 (2) (f) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 where the provision of such summary procedure does not exclude the ordinary procedure in such suits.
Time from which period begins to run: When the debt or liquidated demand becomes payable or when the property becomes recoverable.

Description: Upon a Statute, Act, Regulation or Bye-law, for a penalty or forfeiture.
Time from which period begins to run: When the penalty or forfeiture is incurred.

Description: For the wages of a household servant, artisan or labourer.
Time from which period begins to run: When the wages accrue due.

Description: For the price of food or drink sold by the keeper of a hotel, tavern or lodging-house.
Time from which period begins to run: When the food or drink is delivered.

Description: For the price of lodging.
Time from which period begins to run: When the price becomes payable.

Description: To enforce a right of pre-emption whether the right is founded on law, or general usage, or on special contract.
Time from which period begins to run: When the purchaser takes, under the sale sought to be impeached, physical possession of the whole of the property sold, or, where the subject of the sale does not admit of physical possession, when the instrument of sale is registered.

Description:
By a person, against whom any of the following orders has been made to establish the right which he claims to the property comprised in the order:
(1) Order under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, on a claim preferred to, or an objection made to the attachment of, property attached in execution of a decree;
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the order.

Description: By a person against whom an order has been made under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, upon an application by the holder of a decree for the possession of immoveable property or by the purchaser of such property sold in execution of a decree, complaining of resistance or obstruction to the delivery of possession thereof, or upon an application by any person dispossessed of such property in the delivery of possession thereof to the decree-holder or purchaser, to establish the right which he claims to the present possession of the property comprised in the order.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the order.

Description: To set aside any of the following sales:— (a) sale in execution of a decree of a Civil Court; (b) sale in pursuance of a decree or order of a Collector or other officer of revenue; (c) sale for arrears of Government revenue, or for any demand recoverable as such arrears;
Time from which period begins to run: When the sale is confirmed, or would otherwise have become final and conclusive had no such suit been brought.

Description: To alter or set aside a decision or order of a Civil Court in any proceeding other than a suit.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the final decision or order in the case by a Court competent to determine it finally.

Description: To set aside any act or order of an officer of Government in his official capacity, not herein otherwise expressly provided for.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the act or order.

Description: Against Government to set aside any attachment, lease or transfer of immoveable property by the revenue-authorities for arrears of Government revenue.
Time from which period begins to run: When the attachment, lease or transfer is made.

Description: Against Government to recover money paid under protest in satisfaction of a claim made by the revenue authorities on account of arrears of revenue or on account of demands recoverable as such arrears.
Time from which period begins to run: When the payment is made.

Description: Against Government for compensation for land acquired for public purposes.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of determining the amount of the compensation.

Description: Like suit for compensation when the acquisition is not completed.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the refusal to complete.

Description: For compensation for false imprisonment.
Time from which period begins to run: When the imprisonment ends.

Description: By executors, administrators or representatives, under the Legal Representatives' Suits Act, 1855.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the death of the person wronged.

Description: By executors, administrators or representatives under the Fatal Accidents Act, 1855.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the death of the person killed.

Description: For compensation for any other injury to the person.
Time from which period begins to run: When the injury is committed.

Description: For compensation for a malicious prosecution.
Time from which period begins to run: When the plaintiff is acquitted, or the prosecution is otherwise terminated.

Description: For compensation for libel.
Time from which period begins to run: When the libel is published.

Description: For compensation for slander.
Time from which period begins to run: When the words are spoken or, if the words are not actionable in themselves, when the special damage complained of results.

Description: For compensation for loss of service occasioned by the seduction of the plaintiff’s servant or daughter.
Time from which period begins to run: When the loss occurs.

Description: For compensation for inducing a person to break a contract with the plaintiff.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the breach.

Description: For compensation for and illegal, irregular or excessive distress.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the distress.

Description: For compensation for wrongful seizure of moveable property under legal process.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the seizure.

Description: Against a carrier for compensation for losing or injuring goods.
Time from which period begins to run: When the loss or injury occurs.

Description: Against a carrier for compensation for non- delivery of, or delay in delivering, goods.
Time from which period begins to run: When the goods ought to be delivered.

Description: Against one who, having a right to use property for specific purposes, perverts it to other purposes.
Time from which period begins to run: When the perversion first becomes known to the person injured thereby.

Description: Under the Legal Repre- sentatives’ Suits Act, 1855, against an executor.
Time from which period begins to run: When the wrong complained of is done.

Description: Under the same Act against an administrator.
Time from which period begins to run: Ditto.

Description: Under the same Act against any other representative.
Time from which period begins to run: Ditto.

Description:
For compensation for any malfeasance, misfeasance or non-feasance independent of contract and not herein specially provided for.
*Part VI.—Three years.*
Time from which period begins to run:
When the misfeasance, misfeasance or non-feasance takes place.
*Part VI.—Three years.*

Description: For compensation for obstructing a way or a watercourse.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the obstruction.

Description: For compensation for diverting a water-course.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the diversion.

Description: For compensation for trespass upon immoveable property.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the trespass.

Description: For compensation for infringing copyright or any other exclusive privilege.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the infringement.

Description: To restrain waste.
Time from which period begins to run: When the waste begins.

Description: For compensation for injury caused by an injunction wrongfully obtained.
Time from which period begins to run: When the injunction ceases.

Description: Under the Succession Act, 1925, section 360 or section 361, to compel a refund by a person to whom an executor or administrator has paid a legacy or distributed assets.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the payment or distribution.

Description: By a ward who has attained majority, to set aside a transfer of property by his guardian.
Time from which period begins to run: When the ward attains majority.

Description: By a party bound by such award to recover any property comprised therein.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the final award or order in the case.

Description: By any person bound by an order respecting the possession of immovable property made under the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898, or by any one claiming under such person, to recover the property comprised in such order.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the final order in the case.

Description:
For specific moveable property lost or acquired by theft, or dishonest misappropriation or conversion, or for compensation for wrongfully taking or detaining the same.
1 [48A. To recover moveable property conveyed or bequeathed in trust, deposited or pawned, and afterwards bought from the trustee, depositary or pawnee for a valuable consideration.
Time from which period begins to run:
When the person having the right to the possession of the property first learns in whose possession it is.
When the sale becomes known to the plaintiff.

Description: To set aside sale of moveable property comprised in a Hindu, Muslim or Buddhist religious or charitable endowment, made by a manager thereof for a valuable consideration.
Time from which period begins to run: When the sale becomes known to the plaintiff.]

Description: For other specific moveable property, or for compensation for wrongfully taking or injuring or wrongfully detaining the same.
Time from which period begins to run: When the property is wrongfully taken or injured, or when the detainer’s possession becomes unlawful.

Description: For the hire of animals, vehicles, boats or household furniture.
Time from which period begins to run: When the hire becomes payable.

Description: For the balance of money advanced in payment of goods to be delivered.
Time from which period begins to run: When the goods ought to be delivered.

Description: For the price of goods sold and delivered, where no fixed period of credit is agreed upon.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the delivery of the goods.

Description: For the price of goods sold and delivered to be paid for after the expiry of a fixed period of credit.
Time from which period begins to run: When the period of credit expires.

Description: For the price of goods sold and delivered to be paid for by a bill of exchange, no such bill being given.
Time from which period begins to run: When the period of the proposed bill elapses.

Description: For the price of trees or growing crops sold by the plaintiff to the defendant where no fixed period of credit is agreed upon.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the sale.

Description: For the price of work done by the plaintiff for the defendant at his request, where no time has been fixed for payment.
Time from which period begins to run: When the work is done.

Description: For money payable for money lent.
Time from which period begins to run: When the loan is made.

Description: Like suit when the lender has given a cheque for the money.
Time from which period begins to run: When the cheque is paid.

Description: For money lent under an agreement that it shall be payable on demand.
Time from which period begins to run: When the loan is made.

Description: For money deposited under an agreement that it shall be payable on demand, including money of a customer in the hands of his banker so payable.
Time from which period begins to run: When the demand is made.

Description: For money payable to the plaintiff for money paid for the defendant.
Time from which period begins to run: When the money is paid.

Description: For money payable by the defendant to the plaintiff for money received by the defendant for the plaintiff’s use.
Time from which period begins to run: When the money is received.

Description: For money payable for interest upon money due from the defendant to the plaintiff.
Time from which period begins to run: When the interest becomes due.

Description:
For money payable to the plaintiff for money found to be due from the defendant to the plaintiff on accounts stated between them.
1 [64A. Under Order XXXVII of the Code of Civil 2 [Procedure, 1908.]
Time from which period begins to run:
When the accounts are stated in writing signed by the defendant or his agent duly authorized in this behalf, unless where the debt is, by a simultaneous agreement in writing signed as aforesaid, made payable at a future time, and then when that time arrives.
When the debt becomes payable.]

Description: For compensation for breach of a promise to do anything at a specified time, or upon the happening of a specified contingency.
Time from which period begins to run: When the time specified arrives or the contingency happens.

Description: On a single bond, where a day is specified for payment.
Time from which period begins to run: The day so specified.

Description: On a single bond, where no such day is specified.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of executing the bond.

Description: On a bond subject to a condition.
Time from which period begins to run: When the condition is broken.

Description: On a bill of exchange or promissory note payable at a fixed time after date.
Time from which period begins to run: When the bill or note falls due.

Period of limitation: On a bill of exchange payable at sight or after sight, but not at a fixed time.
Time from which period begins to run: Three years..

Period of limitation: On a bill of exchange accepted payable at a particular place.
Time from which period begins to run: Three years..

Period of limitation: On a bill of exchange or promissory note payable at a fixed time after sight or after demand.
Time from which period begins to run: Three years..

Period of limitation: On a bill of exchange or promissory note payable on demand and not accompanied by any writing restraining or postponing the right to sue.
Time from which period begins to run: Three years..

Period of limitation: On a promissory note or bond payable by instalments.
Time from which period begins to run: Three years..

Period of limitation: On a promissory note or bond payable by instalments, which provides that if default be made in payment of one or more instalments, the whole shall be due.
Time from which period begins to run: Three years..

Period of limitation: On a promissory note given by the maker to a third person to be delivered to the payee after a certain even should happen.
Time from which period begins to run: Three years..

Description: On a dishonoured foreign bill where protest has been made and notice given.
Time from which period begins to run: When the notice is given.

Description: By the payee against the drawer of a bill of exchange which has been dishonoured by non-acceptance.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the refusal to accept.

Description: By the acceptor of an accommodation bill against the drawer.
Time from which period begins to run: When the acceptor pays the amount of the bill.

Description: Suit on a bill of exchange, promissory note or bond not herein expressly provided for.
Time from which period begins to run: When the bill, note or bond becomes payable.

Description: By a surety against the principal debtor.
Time from which period begins to run: When the surety pays the creditor.

Description: By a surety against a co-surety.
Time from which period begins to run: When the surety pays anything in excess of his own share.

Description: Upon any other contract to indemnify.
Time from which period begins to run: When the plaintiff is actually damnified.

Description: By an 1 [advocate] for his costs of a suit or a particular business, there being no express agreement as to the time when such costs are to be paid.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the termination of the Suit or business, or (where the 1 [advocate]) properly discontinues the suit or business) the date of such discontinuance.

Description: For the balance due on a mutual, open and current account, where there have been reciprocal demands between the parties.
Time from which period begins to run: The close of the year in which the last item admitted or proved is entered in the account; such year to be computed as in the account.

Description:
(a) On a policy of insurance when the sum insured is payable after proof of the death has been given to or received by the insurers.
(b) On a policy of insurance when the sum insured is payable after proof of the loss has been given to or received by the insurers.
Time from which period begins to run:
(a) The date of the death of the deceased.
(b) The date of the occurrence causing the loss.

Description: By the assured to recover premia paid under a policy voidable at the election of the insurers.
Time from which period begins to run: When the insurers elect to avoid the policy.

Description: Against a factor for an account.
Time from which period begins to run: When the account is, during the continuance of the agency, demanded and refused or, where no such demand is made, when the agency terminates.

Description: By a principal against his agent for moveable property received by the latter and not accounted for.
Time from which period begins to run: Ditto.

Description: Other suits by principals against agents for neglect or misconduct.
Time from which period begins to run: When the neglect or misconduct becomes known to the plaintiff.

Description: To cancel or set aside an instrument not otherwise provided for.
Time from which period begins to run: When the facts entitling the plaintiff to have the instrument cancelled or set aside become known to him.

Description: To declare the forgery of an instrument issued or registered.
Time from which period begins to run: When the issue or registration becomes known to the plaintiff.

Description: To declare the forgery of an instrument attempted to be enforced against the plaintiff.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the attempt.

Description: For property which the plaintiff has conveyed while insane.
Time from which period begins to run: When the plaintiff is restored to sanity, and has knowledge of the conveyance.

Description: To set aside a decree obtained by fraud, or for other relief on the ground of fraud.
Time from which period begins to run: When the fraud becomes known to the party wronged.

Description: For relief on the ground of mistake.
Time from which period begins to run: When the mistake becomes known to the plaintiff.

Description: For money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the failure.

Description: To make good out of the general estate of a deceased trustee the loss occasioned by a breach of trust.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the trustee's death, or, if the loss has not then resulted, the date of the loss.

Description: For contribution by a party who has paid the whole or more than his share of the amount due under a joint decree, or by a sharer in a joint estate who has paid the whole or more than his share of the amount of revenue due from himself and his co-sharers.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the payment in excess of the plaintiff's own share.

Description: By a co-trustee to enforce against the estate of a deceased trustee a claim for contribution.
Time from which period begins to run: When the right to contribution accrues.

Description: For a seaman’s wages.
Time from which period begins to run: The end of the voyage during which the wages are earned.

Description: For wages not otherwise expressly provided for by this schedule.
Time from which period begins to run: When the wages accrue due.

Description: By a Muslim for exigible dower (*mu’ajjal*).
Time from which period begins to run: When the dower is demanded and refused or (where, during the continuance of the marriage no such demand has been made) when the marriage is dissolved by death or divorce.

Description: By a Muslim for deferred dower (*mu’wajjal*).
Time from which period begins to run: When the marriage is dissolved by death or divorce.

Description: By a mortgagor after the mortgage has been satisfied, to recover surplus collections received by the mortgagee.
Time from which period begins to run: When the mortgagor re-enters on the mortgaged property.

Description: For an account and a share of the profits of a dissolved partnership.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the dissolution.

Description: By the manager of a joint estate of an undivided family for contribution, in respect of a payment made by him on account of the estate.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the payment.

Description: By a lessor for the value of trees cut down by his lessee contrary to the terms of the lease.
Time from which period begins to run: When the trees are cut down.

Description: For the profits of immoveable property belonging to the plaintiff which have been wrongfully received by the defendant.
Time from which period begins to run: When the profits are received.

Description: For arrears of rent ....
Time from which period begins to run: When the arrears become due.

Description: By a vendor of immoveable property for personal payment of unpaid purchase-money.
Time from which period begins to run: The time fixed for completing the sale, or (where the title is accepted after the time fixed for completion) the date of the acceptance.

Description: For a call by a company registered under any Statute or Act.
Time from which period begins to run: When the call is payable.

Description: For specific performance of a contract.
Time from which period begins to run: The date fixed for the performance, or, if no such date is fixed, when the plaintiff has notice that performance is refused.

Description: For the rescission of a contract.
Time from which period begins to run: When the facts entitling the plaintiff to have the contract rescinded first become known to him.

Description: For compensation for the breach of any contract, express or implied, not in writing registered and not herein specially provided for.
Time from which period begins to run: When the contract is broken, or (where there are successive breaches) when the breach in respect of which the suit is instituted occurs, or (where the breach is continuing) when it ceases.

Description: For compensation for the breach of a contract in writing registered.
Time from which period begins to run: When the period of limitation would begin to run against a suit brought on a similar contract not registered.

Description: Upon a foreign judgment as defined in the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the judgment.

Description: To obtain a declaration that an alleged adoption is invalid, or never, in fact, took place.
Time from which period begins to run: When the alleged adoption becomes known to the plaintiff.

Description: To obtain a declaration that an adoption is valid.
Time from which period begins to run: When the rights of the adopted son, as such, are interfered with.

Description:
Suit for which no period of limitation is provided elsewhere in this schedule.
*Part VIII.—Twelve years*
Time from which period begins to run:
When the right to sue accrues.
*Part VIII.—Twelve years*

Description: To avoid incumbrances or under-tenures in an entire estate sold for arrears of Government revenue, or in a *patni taluq* or other saleable tenure sold for arrears of rent.
Time from which period begins to run: When the sale becomes final and conclusive.

Description: Upon a judgment obtained in Bangladesh or a recognisance.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the judgment or recognisance.

Description: For a legacy or for a share of a residue bequeathed by a testator, or for a distributive share of the property of an intestate.
Time from which period begins to run: When the legacy or share becomes payable or deliverable.

Description: For possession of a hereditary office.
Time from which period begins to run: When the defendant takes possession of the office adversely to the plaintiff. *Explanation.*—An hereditary office is possessed when the profits thereof are usually received, or (if there are no profits) when the duties thereof are usually performed.

Description: Suit during the life of a Hindu or Muslim female by a Hindu or Muslim who, if the female died at the date of instituting the suit, would be entitled to the possession of land, to have an alienation of such land made by the female declared to be void except for her life or until her re-marriage.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the alienation.

Description: By a Hindu governed by the law of the Mitakshara to set aside his father’s alienation of ancestral property.
Time from which period begins to run: When the alienee takes possession of the property.

Description: By a person excluded from joint family property to enforce a right to share therein.
Time from which period begins to run: When the exclusion becomes known to the plaintiff.

Description: By a Hindu for arrears of maintenance.
Time from which period begins to run: When the arrears are payable.

Description: By a Hindu for a declaration of his right to maintenance.
Time from which period begins to run: When the right is denied.

Description: For the resumption or assessment of rent-free land.
Time from which period begins to run: When the right to resume or assess the land first accrues.

Description: To establish a periodically recurring right.
Time from which period begins to run: When the plaintiff is first refused the enjoyment of the right.

Description:
To enforce payment of money charged upon immoveable property.
Explanation.—For the purposes of this article—
(a) the allowance and fees respectively called malikana and haqqs, and
(b) the value of any agricultural or other produce the right to receive which is secured by a charge upon immoveable property, and
(c) advances secured by mortgage by deposit of title-deeds,
Time from which period begins to run: When the money sued for becomes due.

Description:
To recover possession of immoveable property conveyed or bequeathed in trust or mortgaged and afterwards transferred by the trustee or mortgagee for a valuable consideration.
1 [134A. To set aside a transfer of immoveable property comprised in a Hindu, Muslim or Buddhist religious or charitable endowment, made by a manager thereof for a valuable consideration.
Time from which period begins to run: When the transfer becomes known to the plaintiff.

Description: By the manager of Hindu, Muslim or Buddhist religious or charitable endowment to recover possession of immoveable property comprised in the endowment, which has been transferred by a previous manager for a valuable consideration.
Time from which period begins to run: The death, resignation or removal of the transferor.

Description: By the manager of Hindu, Muslim or Buddhist religious or charitable endowment to recover possession of moveable property comprised in the endowment which has been sold by a previous manager for a valuable consideration.
Time from which period begins to run: The death, resignation or removal of the seller.]

Description: Suit instituted in a Court other than the High Court Division by a mortgagee for possession of immoveable property mortgaged.
Time from which period begins to run: When the mortgagor’s right to possession determines.

Description: By a purchaser at a private sale for possession of immoveable property sold when the vendor was out of possession at the date of the sale.
Time from which period begins to run: When the vendor is first entitled to possession.

Description: Like suit by a purchaser at a sale in execution of a decree, when the judgment-debtor was out of possession at the date of the sale.
Time from which period begins to run: When the judgment-debtor is first entitled to possession.

Description: Like suit by a purchaser at a sale in execution of a decree, when the judgment-debtor was in possession at the date of the sale.
Time from which period begins to run: The date when the sale becomes absolute.

Period of limitation: By a landlord to recover possessions from a tenant.
Time from which period begins to run: Twelve years.

Period of limitation: By a remainder man, a reversioner (other than a landlord) or a devisee, for possession of immoveable property.
Time from which period begins to run: Twelve years.

Period of limitation: Like suit by a Hindu or Muslim entitled to the possession of immoveable property on the death of a Hindu or Muslim female.
Time from which period begins to run: Twelve years.

Period of limitation: For possession of immoveable property when the plaintiff, while in possession of the property, has been dispossessed or has discontinued the possession.
Time from which period begins to run: Twelve years.

Period of limitation: Like suit, when the plaintiff has become entitled by reason of any forfeiture or breach of condition.
Time from which period begins to run: Twelve years.

Period of limitation: For possession of immoveable property or any interest therein not hereby otherwise specially provided for.
Time from which period begins to run: Twelve years.

Description: Against a depositary or pawnee to recover moveable property deposited or pawned.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the deposit or pawn.

Description: Before the High Court Division in the exercise of its ordinary original civil jurisdiction by a mortgagee to recover from the mortgagor the possession of immoveable property mortgaged.
Time from which period begins to run: When any part of the principal or interest was last paid on account of the mortgage-debt.

Description: By or on behalf of any local authority for possession of any public street or road or any part thereof from which it has been dispossessed or of which it has discontinued the possession.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the dispossession or discontinuance.

Description: By a mortgagee for foreclosure or sale.
Time from which period begins to run: When the money secured by the mortgage becomes due.

Description: Against a mortgagee to redeem or to recover possession of immoveable property mortgaged.
Time from which period begins to run: When the right to redeem or to recover possession accrues: 1 [* * *].

Period of limitation: Any suit by or on behalf of the 1 [Government] except a suit before the 2 [Appellate Division] in the exercise of its original jurisdiction.
Time from which period begins to run: Sixty years.

Period of limitation: Under the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898, from a sentence of death passed by a Court to Session or by the High Court Division in the exercise of its original Criminal Jurisdiction.
Time from which period begins to run: Seven days

Period of limitation: From a decree or order of the High Court Division in the exercise of its original jurisdiction.
Time from which period begins to run: Twenty days

Period of limitation: Under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, to the Court of a District Judge.
Time from which period begins to run: Thirty days

Period of limitation: Under the same Code to the High Court Division from an order of a Subordinate Court refusing leave to appeal to the 2 [Appellate Division].
Time from which period begins to run: Thirty days

Description: Under the Court of Criminal Procedure, 1898, to any Court other than the High Court Division.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the sentence or order appealed from.

Description: Under the same Code to the High Court Division, except in the cases provided for by article 150 and article 157.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the sentence or order appealed from.

Description: Under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, to the High Court Division except in the cases provided for by article 151 and article 153.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the decree or order appealed from.

Description: Under the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898, from an order of acquittal.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the order appealed from.

Description: Under the Arbitration Act, 1940, to set aside an award or to get an award remitted for reconsideration.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of service of the notice of filing of the award.

Description: For leave to appear and defend a suit under summary procedure referred to in section 128 (2) (f) or under Order XXXVII of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
Time from which period begins to run: When the summons is served.

Description: For an order under the same Code, to restore to the file an application for review rejected in consequence of the failure of the applicant to appear when the application was called on for hearing.
Time from which period begins to run: When the application for review is rejected.

Description: For a review of judgment by a Court of Small Causes or by Court invested with the jurisdiction of a Court of Small Causes when exercising that jurisdiction.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the decree or order.

Description: For a review of judgment by the High Court Division in the exercise of its original jurisdiction.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the decree or order.

Description: By a plaintiff, for an order to set aside a dismissal for default of appearance or for failure to pay costs of service of process or to furnish security for costs.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the dismissal.

Description: By a defendant, for an order to set aside a decree passed *ex parte*.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the decree or, where the summons was not duly served, when the applicant has knowledge of the decree.

Description: Under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, by a person dispossessed of immoveable property and disputing the right of the decree holder or purchaser at a sale in execution of a decree to be put into possession.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the dispossession.

Description: Under the same Code to set aside a sale in execution of a decree including any such application by a Judgment-debtor.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the sale.

Description: Complaining of resistance or obstruction to delivery of possession of immoveable property decreed or sold in execution of a decree.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the resistance or obstruction.

Description: For the readmission of an appeal dismissed for want of prosecution.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the dismissal.

Description: For the re-hearing of an appeal heard *exparte*.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the decree in appeal, or, where notice of the appeal was not duly served, when the applicant has knowledge of the decree.

Description: For leave to appeal as a pauper.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the decree appealed from.

Description: Under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, for an order to set aside an abatement.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the abatement.

Description: Under the same Code by the assignee or the receiver of an insolvent plaintiff or appellant for an order to set aside the dismissal of a suit or an appeal.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the order of dismissal.

Description: For a review of judgment except in the cases provided for by article 161 and article 162.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the decree or order.

Description: For the issue of a notice under the same Code, to show cause why any payment made out of Court of any money payable under a decree or any adjustment of the decree should not be recorded as certified.
Time from which period begins to run: When the payment or adjustment is made.

Description: For payment of the amount of a decree by instalments.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the decree.

Description: Under the same Code to have the legal representative of a deceased plaintiff or of a deceased appellant made a party.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the death of the deceased plaintiff or appellant.

Description: Under the same Code to have the legal representative of a deceased defendant or of a deceased respondent made a party.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the death of the deceased defendant or respondent.

Description: Under the Arbitration Act, 1940, for the filing in Court of an award.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of service of the notice of making of the award.

Description: By a person desiring to appeal under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 to the 1 [Appellate Division] for leave to appeal.
Time from which period begins to run: The date of the decree appealed from.

Description: By a purchaser of immoveable property at a sale in execution of a decree for delivery of possession.
Time from which period begins to run: When the sale becomes absolute.

Description: Applications for which no period of limitation is provided elsewhere in this schedule or by section 48 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
Time from which period begins to run: When the right to apply accrues.

Description: For the execution of a decree or order of any Civil Court other than,— (1) applications to which section 48 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, applied; and (2) applications provided for by Article 183.
Period of limitation: Three years; or, where a certified copy of the decree or order has been registered, six years.
Time from which period begins to run: 1. The date of the decree or order, or 2. (where there has been an appeal) the date of the final decree or order of the appellate Court, or the withdrawal of the appeal, or
